[![](https://a.sfdcstatic.com/shared/images/pbc/icons/linkedin.svg)](https://www.linkedin.com/shareArticle?mini=true&url=https://www.salesforce.com/sales/revenue-lifecycle-management/tiered-pricing/&title=Tiered Pricing: How It Works, Types, Examples, and Benefits)\\n* [![](https://a.sfdcstatic.com/shared/images/pbc/icons/x-icon.svg)](https://twitter.com/intent/tweet?text=Tiered Pricing: How It Works, Types, Examples, and Benefits&url=https://www.salesforce.com/sales/revenue-lifecycle-management/tiered-pricing/)\\n* [![](https://a.sfdcstatic.com/shared/images/pbc/icons/facebook.svg)](https://www.facebook.com/sharer/sharer.php?u=https://www.salesforce.com/sales/revenue-lifecycle-management/tiered-pricing/)\\n* ![](https://a.sfdcstatic.com/shared/images/pbc/icons/copy-link.svg)\\n\\n# Tiered Pricing: A Complete Guide\\n\\nTiered pricing models can help you attract more customers, drive upsells, and build predictable revenue without leaving money on the table.\\n\\n[**Star Jacobs**](https://www.salesforce.com/blog/author/star-jacobs/)**, Sales Director at Coastal**\\n\\n*May 13, 2026*\\n\\n* Share the story\\n* [![](https://a.sfdcstatic.com/shared/images/pbc/icons/linkedin.svg)](https://www.linkedin.com/shareArticle?mini=true&url=https://www.salesforce.com/sales/revenue-lifecycle-management/tiered-pricing/&title=Tiered Pricing: How It Works, Types, Examples, and Benefits)\\n* [![](https://a.sfdcstatic.com/shared/images/pbc/icons/x-icon.svg)](https://twitter.com/intent/tweet?text=Tiered Pricing: How It Works, Types, Examples, and Benefits&url=https://www.salesforce.com/sales/revenue-lifecycle-management/tiered-pricing/)\\n* [![](https://a.sfdcstatic.com/shared/images/pbc/icons/facebook.svg)](https://www.facebook.com/sharer/sharer.php?u=https://www.salesforce.com/sales/revenue-lifecycle-management/tiered-pricing/)\\n* ![](https://a.sfdcstatic.com/shared/images/pbc/icons/copy-link.svg)\\n\\n[Watch demo](https://www.salesforce.com/sales/demos/agentforce-revenue-management-demo/?d=sh)\\n\\n#### Key takeaways\\n\\n* Tiered pricing structures products or services into multiple price points based on features, usage, or volume, giving customers options and businesses more revenue potential.\\n* The most effective tiers are clearly named, focused on pain points, and backed by customer usage data, competitive research, and ROI analysis.\\n* Sales reps play a critical role in guiding customers to the right tier by aligning product capabilities with specific business problems.\\n* Tiered pricing supports revenue forecasting and customer retention and provides a built-in roadmap for upselling \u2014 especially in SaaS and subscription models.\\n\\nA prospect lands on your pricing page. They see one option and one price. Seems simple, right? But then they click away. What happened?\\n\\nIn my experience, buyers always do a lot of research and explore several options. If you offer only one solution at one price point, they may look elsewhere to find a better fit for their needs. But if you offer options with different levels of value, buyers may not need to look elsewhere. This is the core benefit of a tiered pricing strategy.\\n\\nIn this guide, learn what tiered pricing is, how it works, models to consider, and best practices for setting it up.\\n\\n## What is tiered pricing?\\n\\nTiered pricing is a strategy that offers a product or service at multiple price points based on specific levels of features, usage, or volume.\\n\\nCharging a flat rate may seem simpler, but tiered pricing lets you tailor offerings to different customer needs, budgets, and business complexities. That means you can reach a wider range of prospects \u2013 from lean startups that need basic tools to global enterprises needing advanced capabilities. Each tier typically delivers more value than the one below it, creating a natural progression that supports both acquisition and [upsells](https://www.salesforce.com/blog/what-is-upselling).\\n\\n[***\u25b8\u25b8\u25b8 See how to manage revenue on one platform***](https://www.salesforce.com/sales/demos/agentforce-revenue-management-demo/?d=pb)\\n\\n *Watch a demo of Revenue Cloud in action and discover how to speed up growth with help from agents.*\\n\\n## How does tiered pricing work?\\n\\nTiered pricing works by segmenting a product\\'s value into distinct packages, each offering different levels of features and functionality. The main thing to remember is that each tier must deliver more perceived value than the one below.\\n\\nYou start by identifying the key features, usage thresholds, or quantities that drive value for different customer segments. Group those elements into tiers \u2014 usually three \u2014 w

Fin and Intercom plans explained\\n\\n# Fin and Intercom plans explained\\n\\nCompare plan pricing, included features, add-ons, and usage-based charges.\\n\\n![](https://static.intercomassets.com/avatars/5355539/square_128/5355539-1665143317.jpg)\\n\\nWritten by Beth-Ann Sher\\n\\nUpdated over a week ago\\n\\nTable of contents\\n\\nThis article refers to our [new pricing plans](https://www.intercom.com/pricing). For information about one of our legacy plans, please log in to view [these articles](https://www.intercom.com/help/en/collections/3920120-intercom-plans) instead, or contact us through the Messenger.\\n\\nOur plans are designed to give you clarity and flexibility as your support needs grow. You can choose from the Essential, Advanced, or Expert plan for Fin and Intercom, and move between them at any time. Each teammate requires a seat, and the plan you select determines the seat price. Advanced and Expert also include lite seats for teammates who need limited access.\\n\\nAll prices are shown in United States Dollars (USD).\\n\\n# Seat pricing\\n\\nThe seat price depends on the plan you choose. You can purchase as many seats as you need (there are no minimums or maximums).\\n\\n|  |  |  |  |\\n| --- | --- | --- | --- |\\n| **Plan** | **Monthly seat price** | **[Annual seat price](https://www.intercom.com/help/en/articles/891-how-to-sign-up-for-a-free-trial-of-intercom#h_244876b58a)** | **Lite seats included** |\\n| **Essential** | $39/seat | $29/seat | No lite seats |\\n| **Advanced** | $99/seat | $85/seat | 20 lite seats included |\\n| **Expert** | $139/seat | $132/seat | 50 lite seats included |\\n\\n# Plan comparison\\n\\n* **Essential** is a great fit for individuals, startups, and small businesses that need core support tools without complexity.\\n* **Advanced** is designed for growing support teams that want stronger automation, deeper reporting, and more ways to collaborate.\\n* **Expert** is ideal for large teams that need advanced security controls, multibrand support, and powerful workflow and workload management features.\\n\\nCore features are listed individually below for easy comparison across plans.\\n\\n|  |  |  |  |\\n| --- | --- | --- | --- |\\n| **Feature** | **Essential** | **Advanced** | **Expert** |\\n| **Fin AI Agent** |
